tasks.title: Implement procedural quest object mesh generator
tasks.description: Create client/src/components/3DGame/ProceduralQuestObjects.ts. Build a procedural mesh generator for quest-related objects that we don't have real assets for yet. Generate simple colored primitives: spheres for fruits/balls, boxes for packages/books, cylinders for bottles/containers, combined primitives for furniture/tools. Each object type should have a color, label billboard, and optional glow highlight. Accept parameters: objectType (from a registry of ~30 common nouns like 'apple', 'book', 'key', 'bottle', 'ball', 'chair'), color (for visual vocabulary quests), size, and interactable flag. Add a pulsing outline shader or glow layer to distinguish quest objects from environment objects. These are temporary placeholders — design the interface so real GLTF models can replace them later via an asset registry lookup.

tasks.title: Create quest content seed library with parameterized templates
tasks.description: Expand shared/language/quest-templates.ts with a comprehensive seed library of 50+ parameterized quest templates organized by the user's quest categories. Templates should cover: (1) Follow Instructions — 'Have N conversations with X character', 'Go to restaurant and order food', 'Go to hotel and book a room', 'Ask for directions to X', 'Go to X place and talk to Y person about Z'; (2) Visual Vocabulary Nouns/Adjectives — 'Find the {{adjective}} {{noun}} and return it to {{npc}}' with vocabulary_set params for color+object combos; (3) Visual Vocabulary Verbs/Adverbs — 'Find the person who is {{adverb}} {{verb}}ing' with activity descriptions; (4) Listening Comprehension — 'Listen to {{npc}}'s story and report back to {{npc2}} about {{topic}}'; (5) Navigate the City — multi-waypoint chains 'Go to X, then Y, then Z'; (6) Navigate Time — 'Do X thing at Y time'; (7) Vocabulary Bank Collection — 'Find and collect N {{category}} words by interacting with objects'. Each template must specify: questType, difficulty range, objectiveTypes (from the existing 16 types in shared/quest-types/language-learning.ts), estimated XP reward, required vocabulary categories, and placeholder parameters for AI fill-in.

tasks.title: Implement quest assignment engine that generates real playable quests from templates
tasks.description: Create server/services/quest-assignment-engine.ts. This is the core system that takes quest templates and the current world state (available NPCs, locations, vocabulary categories, player CEFR level) and produces concrete, playable quest instances. On world load or when a player needs new quests, the engine should: (1) query player's current CEFR level, vocabulary mastery, and completed quests; (2) select appropriate templates matching player level (A1 gets simple 'greet NPC' quests, B2 gets complex multi-stage chains); (3) fill template parameters by picking actual NPCs, locations, and vocabulary from the world data; (4) create quest records via the existing POST /api/worlds/:worldId/quests endpoint with fully populated objectives, completionCriteria, locationId, and assignedTo fields; (5) ensure variety — don't repeat the same template type consecutively. Generate 3-5 active quests at a time. Wire this into the game startup flow so players always have quests available.

tasks.title: Implement server-side SSE streaming for Gemini chat endpoint
Implement server-side sentence boundary detection during streaming
tasks.description: Create server/services/sentence-splitter.ts. During SSE streaming, buffer incoming tokens and detect sentence boundaries (period, exclamation, question mark followed by space/newline/EOF). When a sentence is complete, emit a 'data: {"sentence": "...", "sentenceIndex": N}\n\n' SSE event in addition to the text token events. This enables sentence-level TTS pipelining on the client.

tasks.title: Create stripSystemMarkers utility function
tasks.description: Add export function stripSystemMarkers(text: string): string to shared/language-progress.ts. Should remove **GRAMMAR_FEEDBACK**...**END_GRAMMAR** blocks, **QUEST_ASSIGN**...**END_QUEST** blocks, and also handle partial/incomplete blocks (important during streaming where a block may be mid-transmission). Use regex with global flag. Also strip any orphaned markers like standalone **GRAMMAR_FEEDBACK** or **END_GRAMMAR** lines.

tasks.title: Implement browser Web Speech API for instant STT
tasks.description: Create client/src/lib/browser-speech.ts with a BrowserSpeechRecognizer class wrapping webkitSpeechRecognition/SpeechRecognition. Features: start/stop recording, real-time interim results callback, final transcript callback, language setting via BCP-47 code, error handling with fallback flag. Export an isSupported() check. This provides instant transcription with zero network latency (2-4 second improvement over Gemini STT).

tasks.title: Implement TTS response caching with LRU
tasks.description: Create server/services/tts-cache.ts with an LRU cache for TTS results. Key: SHA-256 hash of (text + voiceName + languageCode). Value: audio Buffer. Max size: 200 entries or 100MB (whichever is hit first). Wrap the textToSpeech() calls in /api/tts and the returnAudio path to check cache first. Add cache hit/miss logging. Common phrases (greetings, farewells, short NPC responses) will be served instantly on repeat.

tasks.title: Implement conversation context caching on server
tasks.description: Create server/services/chat-session-manager.ts. Maintain an in-memory map of active chat sessions keyed by (worldId + npcId + playerId). Each session stores the Gemini chat object (from model.startChat()) so subsequent messages don't re-send the full conversation history. Sessions expire after 10 minutes of inactivity. Add sessionId to the /api/gemini/chat request/response so the client can reuse sessions. This reduces token usage and latency for multi-turn conversations.

tasks.title: [US-067] Intra-settlement street network topology generation
tasks.description: As a developer, I need the geography generator (server/generators/geography-generator.ts) to produce actual street segments with start/end world-space coordinates within each settlement, replacing the current metadata-only street entries. Use a configurable layout algorithm (grid for planned towns, organic/radial for older villages) seeded by settlement type and founding era. Store street segments as polylines with intersection nodes so downstream systems can render roads and place buildings along them. This is a prerequisite for US-035, US-036, US-040, US-045, US-048, and US-056.

tasks.title: [US-027] Settlement elevation profile
tasks.description: As a developer, I need settlement-level elevation metadata so maps and UIs can display elevation information.

tasks.title: [US-033] Water feature schema and IR types
tasks.description: As a developer, I need schema and IR types for all water features so they persist and export correctly.

tasks.title: [US-049] Poisson disc sampling module
tasks.description: As a developer, I need a Poisson disc sampling implementation for natural-looking spacing of vegetation and objects.

tasks.title: [US-050] Biome-elevation vegetation zones
tasks.description: As a developer, I need a vegetation zone system mapping biome, elevation, and moisture to plant species.

tasks.title: Implement 'Follow Instructions' quest completion detection
tasks.description: Wire up end-to-end completion detection for 'Follow Instructions' quests in UtteranceQuestSystem.ts and QuestPostConditionValidator.ts. These quests require the player to perform specific actions: (1) 'Have N conversations with character X' — listen for conversation_turn events on GameEventBus, count turns with the specified NPC, mark objective complete when threshold reached; (2) 'Go to location and do action' — combine visit_location objective (detected via player proximity to locationPosition) with conversation or interaction objectives; (3) 'Ask for directions' — detect when player uses direction-related vocabulary in conversation with any NPC. For each sub-type, update quest.progress in real-time, emit quest progress events, and when all objectives are met, set quest.status='completed', fire quest_completed event, and award XP. The existing QuestPostConditionValidator already checks target_language_pct and grammar_accuracy — integrate these as bonus scoring criteria.

tasks.title: Implement 'Visual Vocabulary' quest completion detection
tasks.description: Wire up completion detection for Visual Vocabulary quests. Two sub-types: (1) Nouns/Adjectives — QuestObjectManager spawns a procedural quest object (from ProceduralQuestObjects.ts) matching the quest description (e.g., 'red bowling ball') at a random location in the settlement. Player must find it (proximity detection), interact with it (click/tap), and the system validates they can name it in the target language via a quick prompt ('What is this? Answer in [language]'). On correct answer, the object is 'collected' and player must return it to the quest-giving NPC. (2) Verbs/Adverbs — spawn an NPC performing a specific animation/activity at a location. Player must find the NPC, observe the action, and describe it in the target language ('What is this person doing?'). Validate the response contains the target verb/adverb. Use UtteranceQuestSystem's existing speak_phrase and use_vocabulary objective types. Update quest progress on each successful identification.

tasks.title: Implement 'Listening Comprehension' quest completion detection
tasks.description: Wire up completion detection for Listening Comprehension quests. Flow: (1) Player goes to NPC A (quest giver) who tells a short story in the target language via the chat system — the story is generated by Gemini with controlled vocabulary appropriate to the player's CEFR level; (2) Player must then go to NPC B and relay key facts from the story. The system should generate 2-3 comprehension check questions that NPC B asks (e.g., 'What color was the cat?', 'Where did Maria go?'). Use the existing conversation system — inject comprehension questions into NPC B's system prompt. Evaluate player responses via Gemini (send the original story + player's answers, ask Gemini to score comprehension 0-100). Mark quest complete if comprehension score >= 60%. Store the story text and comprehension score in quest.progress for review.

tasks.title: Implement 'Navigate the City' and 'Navigate Time' quest completion detection
tasks.description: Wire up completion detection for navigation quests. (1) Navigate the City — quest specifies an ordered list of 3-5 locations. QuestWaypointManager already creates waypoint beacons — extend it to show waypoints sequentially (only show the next waypoint after the current one is reached). Detect arrival via player position within 15 units of the waypoint. On arrival at each waypoint, play a completion chime, update quest.progress with the checkpoint, and reveal the next waypoint. On final waypoint, mark quest complete. (2) Navigate Time — quest specifies an action to perform at a specific in-game time. Check GameMenuSystem or add a simple day/night cycle time tracker. When the player performs the action (visits location, talks to NPC) within the correct time window, mark complete. If the player misses the window, the quest should indicate 'try again tomorrow' rather than failing. Both quest types should show remaining waypoints/time in the BabylonQuestTracker panel.

tasks.title: Implement server-side per-sentence TTS during streaming
tasks.description: When returnAudio=true AND stream=true, fire off a TTS request for each completed sentence asynchronously. Send the base64 audio in an SSE event: 'data: {"sentenceAudio": "...", "sentenceIndex": N}\n\n'. Use Promise.all-style concurrency so sentence 2 TTS starts while sentence 1 TTS is in flight. Strip system markers (GRAMMAR_FEEDBACK, QUEST_ASSIGN) from each sentence before TTS. Accept languageCode from the request body and pass it through.

tasks.title: Wire BabylonChatPanel streaming to use sentence-level audio queue
tasks.description: Update BabylonChatPanel.ts sendToGeminiStreaming() to handle the new SSE event types: 'sentenceAudio' events should be decoded and pushed into this.audioQueue with their sentenceIndex. Start playAudioQueue() as soon as the first sentenceAudio arrives (don't wait for full response). The playAudioQueue() method already exists and handles sequential playback — just ensure it's triggered early. Strip system markers from partial text during streaming using stripSystemMarkers() on each chunk callback.

tasks.title: Wire CharacterChatDialog to support streaming responses
tasks.description: Add streaming support to CharacterChatDialog.tsx. When sending a message, use fetch with stream: true and read the response body as SSE. Update the assistant message content progressively as text chunks arrive (strip system markers in real-time). Play sentence audio as it arrives instead of waiting for the full response. Add a streaming state indicator (animated dots or similar) while response is in progress.

tasks.title: Implement client-side Voice Activity Detection (VAD)
tasks.description: Enhance client/src/lib/audio-utils.ts with a VoiceActivityDetector class using Web Audio API AnalyserNode. Features: continuous RMS energy monitoring during recording, auto-detect speech start (energy rises above threshold), auto-detect speech end (energy stays below threshold for 1.5 seconds), onSpeechStart/onSpeechEnd callbacks. This enables hands-free recording — user just talks without pressing buttons.

tasks.title: Implement Gemini native audio I/O for single-call voice chat
tasks.description: Investigate and implement Gemini 2.5's native audio input + audio output capability. In server/services/tts-stt.ts or a new server/services/gemini-voice.ts, create a function that sends audio input to Gemini with the system prompt and gets both text and audio output in a single generateContent call. Use model 'gemini-2.5-flash' or 'gemini-2.5-pro' with audio modality config. If the API supports it, this collapses STT+LLM+TTS into one network call. Wire it into the voice-chat endpoint as the preferred path with fallback to the 3-step pipeline.

tasks.title: [US-068] Intra-settlement street rendering in 3D
tasks.description: As a developer, I need the Babylon.js game to render street geometry within settlements as proper roads. Currently RoadGenerator.ts only generates inter-settlement MST roads, and generateSettlementRoads() creates narrow 1.5-unit paths from center to buildings. Replace this with a system that reads the street segment topology from US-067 and renders multi-lane, terrain-following road meshes within the settlement, including intersections, sidewalks, and street name signs at key points.

tasks.title: [US-026] Elevation-aware building placement
tasks.description: As a developer, I need buildings placed with terrain-adaptive foundations so buildings on slopes don't float or clip.

tasks.title: [US-028] River generation
tasks.description: As a developer, I need procedural river generation that carves realistic waterways through terrain.

tasks.title: [US-030] Lake generation
tasks.description: As a developer, I need procedural lake generation from terrain basins.

tasks.title: [US-051] Terrain-aware vegetation placement
tasks.description: As a developer, I need vegetation placed according to terrain data with ecological coherence.

tasks.title: [US-052] Agricultural zone generation
tasks.description: As a developer, I need agricultural areas near settlements with farmland, orchards, and pastures.

tasks.title: Implement quest turn-in and completion ceremony
tasks.description: Create a unified quest completion flow in client/src/components/3DGame/QuestCompletionManager.ts. When a quest's status changes to 'completed': (1) Play a completion sound effect (use AudioContext to generate a simple ascending tone sequence as placeholder); (2) Show a full-screen completion overlay for 3 seconds with quest title, XP earned, and any items/unlocks received — create this as a Babylon.js GUI panel with gold border, quest icon, and animated XP counter; (3) If the quest has itemRewards, add them to playerProgress.inventory; (4) Award experienceReward XP via the gamification system (shared/language/gamification.ts calculateLevel); (5) Check and trigger any achievement unlocks (e.g., 'Quest Hero' for 10 quests); (6) Update playerProgress.questsCompleted array; (7) Remove quest from BabylonQuestTracker's active list and add to completed list; (8) If the quest is part of a questChain, auto-assign the next quest in the chain. Fire all relevant GameEventBus events.

tasks.title: Enhance BabylonQuestTracker with rich quest log UI
tasks.description: Upgrade BabylonQuestTracker.ts to show a full quest log experience. Currently it shows a basic list — enhance it to: (1) Show per-objective progress bars (e.g., '2/3 conversations completed') by reading quest.progress and quest.objectives; (2) Color-code quests by type (conversation=blue, vocabulary=green, navigation=orange, listening=purple, cultural=red); (3) Show quest difficulty stars (1-3) based on quest.difficulty; (4) Add a 'Completed' tab showing finished quests with completion date and rewards earned; (5) Show quest location name and distance from player; (6) Add a 'Track' button that activates QuestWaypointManager for the selected quest; (7) Show a quest description panel when a quest is selected with full objective details. Use Babylon.js GUI AdvancedDynamicTexture. The panel should be toggleable with the J key.

tasks.title: Wire QuestIndicatorManager to show indicators on quest-relevant NPCs
tasks.description: QuestIndicatorManager.ts already has the code to show !/? indicators above NPCs but needs to be wired to actual quest data. On game load and whenever quests change: (1) Query all active quests for the current player; (2) For quests with assignedBy (quest-giving NPC), show a yellow '!' if the quest is available but not yet accepted; (3) For quests with objectives targeting specific NPCs (talk_to_npc, complete_conversation), show a blue '?' on those NPCs; (4) For completed quests that require turn-in to the assignedBy NPC, show a green checkmark; (5) Update indicators in real-time as quest progress changes — listen for quest_accepted, quest_completed, and utterance_quest_progress events on GameEventBus. The indicators should bob up and down and be visible from a distance (billboard mode, scale with distance).

tasks.title: Add quest notification toasts and HUD indicators
tasks.description: Create client/src/components/3DGame/QuestNotificationSystem.ts. Show non-intrusive toast notifications for quest events: (1) 'New Quest Available!' when a quest is assigned — slide in from right, show quest title and type icon, auto-dismiss after 4 seconds; (2) 'Objective Complete!' when a quest objective is finished — brief flash with objective text; (3) 'Quest Complete!' with XP amount when all objectives are met; (4) 'Achievement Unlocked!' with achievement name and icon; (5) 'Level Up!' when XP crosses a level threshold. Also add a persistent HUD element in the top-right corner showing: active quest count badge, current XP/next level progress bar (thin horizontal bar), and a flashing indicator when a quest can be turned in. Use Babylon.js GUI with animations (fade in/out, slide). Integrate with GameEventBus events.

tasks.title: Send final cleaned response and grammar metadata in streaming done event
tasks.description: After streaming completes, send a final SSE event: 'data: {"done": true, "fullText": "...", "rawText": "..."}\n\n' containing both the stripped display text and the raw text (for grammar feedback parsing on the client). This lets the client do a final consistency check and parse grammar feedback from the complete response.

tasks.title: Wire CharacterChatDialog to use combined voice-chat endpoint
tasks.description: When recording completes in CharacterChatDialog, send the audio blob directly to /api/gemini/voice-chat instead of making 3 separate calls (stt -> chat -> tts). Parse the combined response to get transcript, display text, and audio. Show the transcript as user message, the cleaned response as assistant message, and play the audio. Fall back to the 3-call approach if the voice-chat endpoint fails.

tasks.title: Wire BabylonChatPanel to use combined voice-chat endpoint
tasks.description: Update BabylonChatPanel's mediaRecorder.onstop handler to POST the audio blob to /api/gemini/voice-chat instead of calling speechToText() then sendToGemini() separately. Parse the combined response, display transcript and cleaned response, play audio. Preserve grammar feedback parsing and vocabulary tracking. Fall back to separate calls on failure.

tasks.title: Improve silence detection with audio trimming
tasks.description: Enhance isSilentAudio() in client/src/lib/audio-utils.ts to also trim leading and trailing silence from audio blobs before sending to STT. Create a trimSilence(audioBlob, threshold) function that uses AudioContext to find the first/last samples above the energy threshold, then slice the blob. Shorter audio = faster STT transcription and fewer hallucinations. Return the trimmed blob alongside the isSilent boolean.

tasks.title: Add language hint to STT for improved accuracy
tasks.description: Update server/services/tts-stt.ts speechToText() to accept an optional languageHint parameter. When provided, change the Gemini STT prompt from 'Generate a transcript of this audio.' to 'Generate a transcript of this audio. The speaker is likely speaking [language]. Transcribe in the original language, do not translate.' Pass languageHint from the /api/stt endpoint (add languageCode to the request body) and from the voice-chat endpoint. This improves STT accuracy for non-English audio.

tasks.title: Implement conversation history compression for long chats
tasks.description: In server/services/chat-session-manager.ts, when conversation history exceeds 20 messages, compress older messages into a summary. Use a quick Gemini call to summarize the first N messages into 2-3 sentences, then replace those messages with the summary. This prevents context window overflow and keeps token costs manageable for long NPC conversations. Preserve the most recent 10 messages in full.

tasks.title: [US-069] Street-aligned building placement
tasks.description: As a developer, I need buildings placed along street edges instead of on a jittered grid from settlement center. Replace the current WorldScaleManager.generateLotPositions() grid approach in BabylonGame.ts with a system that distributes lots along both sides of each street segment from US-067. House numbers should map to position along the street (odd on one side, even on the other). Commercial buildings cluster near intersections and along main streets; residential buildings fill side streets. This bridges the gap between the backend address model (district→street→lot) and the 3D layout.

tasks.title: [US-029] River crossing points
tasks.description: As a developer, I need river crossing points (bridges, fords) generated where streets intersect rivers.

tasks.title: [US-031] Coastline and bay generation
tasks.description: As a developer, I need coastline and bay shapes for coastal settlements.

tasks.title: [US-053] Rock and geological feature placement
tasks.description: As a developer, I need rocks and geological features placed based on terrain.

tasks.title: Implement vocabulary bank collection mechanic in 3D world
tasks.description: Create client/src/components/3DGame/VocabularyCollectionSystem.ts. This system lets players collect vocabulary words by interacting with objects in the 3D world. (1) Tag world objects (buildings, trees, cars, NPCs, quest objects) with vocabulary metadata: the word in target language, category (noun/verb/adjective/adverb), and difficulty level. (2) When the player approaches a tagged object (within 5 units), show a floating label in the target language above it with a 'collect' prompt. (3) On interaction (click), show a mini-quiz: display the target language word and 3-4 multiple choice options for the meaning (or vice versa for harder difficulties). (4) On correct answer, add the word to the player's vocabulary bank (shared/language/progress.ts VocabularyEntry) with timesEncountered incremented, play a collection particle effect, and award 3 XP (vocabularyNewWord). (5) On incorrect answer, show the correct answer, still add to vocabulary bank as 'new' mastery, no XP. (6) Track which objects the player has already collected words from — don't repeat. Categories to support: nouns (building, tree, car, restaurant, hotel, park), verbs (walking, talking, dancing, eating, reading), adjectives (red, tall, round, big, small), adverbs (slowly, quickly, carefully).

tasks.title: Create vocabulary bank quest objectives integration
tasks.description: Integrate the VocabularyCollectionSystem with the quest system. (1) Add a new objective type 'collect_vocabulary' to the UtteranceQuestSystem that tracks how many words of a specific category the player has collected (e.g., 'Collect 5 food-related nouns'); (2) Create quest templates for vocabulary collection quests: 'Word Explorer: Nouns' (find and collect 5 noun words), 'Color Hunter' (find 3 objects matching color adjectives), 'Action Spotter' (find 3 NPCs doing actions and learn the verbs); (3) When a vocabulary collection quest is active, VocabularyCollectionSystem should highlight uncollected objects matching the quest's target category with a subtle glow or sparkle effect; (4) Update quest progress in real-time as words are collected; (5) On quest completion, grant bonus XP and mark words as 'learning' mastery level. Wire to BabylonVocabularyPanel.ts so collected words appear immediately in the vocabulary bank UI.

tasks.title: Implement vocabulary review and mastery reinforcement
tasks.description: Enhance vocabulary mastery tracking by adding spaced repetition prompts. In VocabularyCollectionSystem.ts: (1) When a player approaches an object they've already collected the word for, occasionally (25% chance) trigger a quick recall quiz — show the object and ask the player to type/say the word in the target language. Correct answer increments timesUsedCorrectly and may upgrade mastery (new→learning after 3 correct, learning→familiar after 5, familiar→mastered after 8). (2) Add a 'Vocabulary Review' quest type that asks the player to revisit 5 previously-learned words and recall them — targets words with lowest mastery or oldest lastEncountered date. (3) In BabylonVocabularyPanel.ts, add a 'Review Due' counter showing words that haven't been reviewed recently. (4) Mastered words should have their world labels displayed in target language only (no translation), encouraging immersion.

tasks.title: Implement combined /api/gemini/voice-chat endpoint
tasks.description: Create a new POST /api/gemini/voice-chat endpoint in server/routes.ts that accepts multipart audio upload + JSON metadata (systemPrompt, messages, voice, languageCode, gameType). Performs STT -> LLM -> TTS all server-side in a single request. Returns JSON with {transcript, response, cleanedResponse, audio (base64), grammarFeedback}. This eliminates 2 network round-trips for voice chat (client currently makes 3 separate calls: /api/stt, /api/gemini/chat, /api/tts).

tasks.title: Integrate browser Web Speech API into BabylonChatPanel
tasks.description: In BabylonChatPanel.ts startRecording(), try BrowserSpeechRecognizer first (if supported). Show interim results in the input text area as the user speaks (real-time feedback). On final result, send the transcript to Gemini chat. Fall back to MediaRecorder + /api/stt if browser speech is unavailable or returns an error. Set the recognition language from world targetLanguage using getLanguageBCP47().

tasks.title: Integrate browser Web Speech API into CharacterChatDialog
tasks.description: In CharacterChatDialog.tsx startRecording(), try BrowserSpeechRecognizer first. Show interim results in the textarea as the user speaks. On final result, auto-send to Gemini. Fall back to MediaRecorder + /api/stt. Set recognition language from worldLangContext.targetLanguage.

tasks.title: Add NPC emotional tone to TTS voice parameters
tasks.description: Enhance TTS to convey NPC emotion. In the Gemini system prompt, ask the model to include a short emotion tag at the start of each sentence (e.g., [happy], [curious], [stern]). Parse these tags server-side before TTS and map them to SSML prosody parameters (pitch, rate, volume adjustments). Strip the tags from display text. This makes NPC speech more expressive and natural-sounding.

tasks.title: [US-070] Address-to-position mapping and lot coordinate persistence
tasks.description: As a developer, I need each Lot record to store its resolved 3D coordinates (positionX, positionY, positionZ) and street-facing rotation so that addresses correspond to physical locations. Add these fields to the lots schema in shared/schema.ts and the Mongo storage layer. Provide a lookup function addressToVector3(settlementId, streetName, houseNumber) that returns the world-space position. This enables NPCs to navigate to addresses, the UI to show 'go to address' waypoints, and ensures consistency between the data model and the visual world.

tasks.title: [US-035] Street-facing building orientation
tasks.description: As a developer, I need buildings oriented to face their assigned street.

tasks.title: [US-036] Corner building handling
tasks.description: As a developer, I need corner buildings at intersections handled specially so they face the correct street.

tasks.title: [US-032] Harbor and dock generation
tasks.description: As a developer, I need harbors and docks generated for coastal and river settlements.

tasks.title: [US-034] Water rendering in 3D
tasks.description: As a developer, I need water features rendered in the Babylon.js 3D engine.

tasks.title: [US-037] Building scale by zone
tasks.description: As a developer, I need building size and style to vary by urban zone.

tasks.title: [US-038] Terrain-adaptive foundation rendering
tasks.description: As a developer, I need visible foundation meshes beneath buildings on slopes.

tasks.title: Wire XP awards to all quest and learning activities
tasks.description: Ensure XP is consistently awarded across all activities using the gamification system. In a new client/src/components/3DGame/XPManager.ts: (1) Listen for GameEventBus events and award XP per shared/language/gamification.ts XP_REWARDS: quest_completed→25, vocabulary_used (new word)→3, vocabulary mastered→15, grammar correct→2, conversation_turn→10, achievement_unlocked→50; (2) After awarding XP, call calculateLevel() to check for level-ups; (3) On level-up, emit a 'level_up' event for the notification system, check skill tree unlocks, and persist to playerProgress via PUT /api/player-progress/:id; (4) Track daily challenge progress — check if the day's challenge criteria are met after each XP-granting event; (5) Maintain a session XP counter for the playerSessions record. Ensure XP is persisted server-side so it survives page refreshes.

tasks.title: Wire achievement detection to gameplay events
tasks.description: Create client/src/components/3DGame/AchievementManager.ts to monitor gameplay and unlock achievements. Listen to GameEventBus events and check achievement conditions from shared/language/gamification.ts ACHIEVEMENTS: (1) 'First Words' — vocabulary.length >= 10; (2) 'Chatterbox' — totalConversations >= 25; (3) 'Grammar Guru' — grammar patterns mastered >= 5; (4) 'Polyglot' — level >= 11; (5) 'Navigator' — navigation quests completed >= 5; (6) 'Streak Master' — streakDays >= 7; (7) 'Word Collector' — vocabulary.length >= 50; (8) 'Quest Hero' — questsCompleted.length >= 10; (9) 'Cultural Explorer' — cultural quests >= 5; (10) 'Bookworm' — articles read >= 10. On unlock: persist to playerProgress.achievementsUnlocked, persist to achievements collection via API, award 50 XP, fire achievement_unlocked event for the notification toast. Check conditions after every relevant event (quest_completed, vocabulary_used, conversation_turn, etc.).

tasks.title: Enhance BabylonSkillTreePanel with interactive visualization
tasks.description: Upgrade BabylonSkillTreePanel.ts from its current state to a polished skill tree visualization. Using Babylon.js GUI: (1) Render the 5 tiers from shared/language/language-skill-tree.ts as a vertical tree with connecting lines between tiers; (2) Each node shows: skill name, icon (use unicode symbols as placeholders), progress bar toward unlock condition, locked/unlocked/completed state with distinct visual treatment (greyed out / glowing border / gold fill); (3) On hover/click, show a tooltip with the unlock condition description and current progress (e.g., 'Learn 20 words — 14/20'); (4) Animate newly unlocked nodes with a burst effect; (5) Show the player's current tier prominently at the top; (6) Connect skill tree unlocks to tangible rewards — when a skill unlocks, grant the player something: new quest types available, new vocabulary categories, access to new areas, or cosmetic items. Persist unlock state in playerProgress.

tasks.title: Implement audio-level pronunciation scoring
tasks.description: Enhance shared/pronunciation-scoring.ts to support audio-level comparison. Create a server endpoint POST /api/pronunciation/score that accepts two audio blobs (reference TTS + player recording). Use Google Cloud Speech-to-Text with word-level timestamps and confidence scores to compare pronunciation accuracy at the phoneme level. Return per-word scores, timing accuracy, and overall pronunciation grade. Fall back to the existing text-based Levenshtein scoring if audio analysis fails.

tasks.title: Precompute NPC greeting audio on chat open
tasks.description: In both BabylonChatPanel and CharacterChatDialog, when the chat dialog opens and the greeting text is generated, immediately fire an async TTS request for the greeting in the background. Store the resulting audio blob. When the user first sees the chat, the greeting audio is already available to play instantly instead of requiring a TTS round-trip. Use the correct languageCode from world context.

tasks.title: Create WebSocket infrastructure for real-time voice chat
tasks.description: Create server/services/websocket-voice.ts with a WebSocket server (using 'ws' package) attached to the Express HTTP server. Protocol: client connects with worldId/npcId/playerId params. Client sends binary audio chunks (16-bit PCM at 16kHz). Server buffers chunks, runs VAD to detect utterance boundaries, then processes complete utterances through STT->LLM->TTS pipeline. Server sends back: interim transcripts (JSON), final transcripts (JSON), response text (JSON), audio chunks (binary). This is the foundation for <1s latency voice chat.

tasks.title: [US-071] Society editor map consistency with 3D game layout
tasks.description: As a developer, I need the Society editor's settlement map preview (GeographyMap.tsx / LocationMapPreview.tsx) to render using the same street layout data produced by US-067, so the 2D editor map matches what the player sees in the 3D game. Extract the street-segment-to-canvas rendering into a shared utility that both the editor and the minimap (US-048) can consume. Display streets, lot positions, building footprints, and address labels so world authors can verify layout correctness before entering the game.

tasks.title: [US-063] Settlement growth mechanics
tasks.description: As a developer, I need settlements to grow physically when population increases during simulation.

tasks.title: [US-064] Settlement decline mechanics
tasks.description: As a developer, I need settlements to show physical decline when population drops.

tasks.title: [US-065] Infrastructure development
tasks.description: As a developer, I need settlement infrastructure to upgrade as the town grows and prospers.

tasks.title: [US-066] Settlement history tracking
tasks.description: As a developer, I need a historical record of settlement changes over simulation time.

tasks.title: [US-054] Nature object LOD system
tasks.description: As a developer, I need level-of-detail rendering for nature objects for performance.

tasks.title: Implement adaptive quest difficulty based on player skill level
tasks.description: Enhance the quest assignment engine (server/services/quest-assignment-engine.ts) to adapt to player performance. (1) Track quest completion rate, average comprehension scores, vocabulary quiz accuracy, and time-to-complete for each quest type; (2) If a player completes 3+ quests of a type with >80% accuracy, increase difficulty: more vocabulary required, longer conversations, harder grammar patterns, tighter time windows; (3) If a player fails or abandons 2+ quests, decrease difficulty: simpler vocabulary, shorter objectives, more hints enabled; (4) Map CEFR levels to quest parameters: A1 gets single-objective quests with common vocabulary (greetings, numbers), A2 gets 2-objective quests with food/family vocab, B1 gets multi-stage chains with all categories, B2 gets quests requiring sustained target-language-only conversation; (5) Store difficulty adjustment factor in playerProgress.stats and use it when generating new quests. The system should feel like it's meeting the player where they are.

tasks.title: Implement quest chain progression for multi-stage learning arcs
tasks.description: Build out quest chains that create structured learning progressions. In server/services/quest-chain-generator.ts (enhance existing): (1) Define 5 pre-built quest chain templates: 'Welcome to Town' (5 quests: greet NPCs → navigate to key locations → order food → book hotel → ask for directions), 'Word Collector' (3 quests: collect 5 nouns → collect 5 adjectives → collect 5 verbs), 'The Town Mystery' (4 quests: listen to story → find clues by talking to NPCs → report findings → solve mystery with vocabulary), 'Market Day' (3 quests: learn food words → go shopping with vocab requirements → describe purchases to NPC), 'Time Traveler' (3 quests: morning routine → afternoon errands → evening social event, each at specific times); (2) Each quest in a chain unlocks the next via prerequisiteQuestIds; (3) Chain completion awards bonus XP (sum of individual quest XP × 1.5) and a special achievement; (4) Show chain progress in BabylonQuestTracker as a progress bar across the chain.

tasks.title: Implement NPC activity system for verb/adverb visual vocabulary quests
tasks.description: Create client/src/components/3DGame/NPCActivitySystem.ts to make NPCs perform observable activities for visual vocabulary quests. Since we lack animation assets, represent activities with: (1) Positional context — NPC stands near relevant objects (chef near kitchen counter, reader near bookshelf); (2) Floating activity icon above NPC — use unicode/emoji text billboards as placeholders (knife icon for 'cutting', book icon for 'reading', musical note for 'singing'); (3) Activity description text visible on approach — '[NPC name] appears to be doing something...'; (4) When player interacts during a verb/adverb quest, prompt: 'What is this person doing? Describe in [target language]'; (5) Define 15 base activities with vocabulary: cooking/cutting/eating (kitchen), reading/writing/studying (library), walking/running/dancing (park), talking/singing/laughing (plaza), buying/selling/counting (market); (6) Each activity has an adverb modifier: slowly, quickly, carefully, loudly, quietly. Assign activities to NPCs based on their occupation and current location.

tasks.title: Add pronunciation practice mode to chat panels
tasks.description: Extend the existing practicePronunciation() in BabylonChatPanel.ts to use audio-level scoring. After the player records their attempt, send both the TTS reference audio and the player's recording to /api/pronunciation/score. Display visual feedback: green/yellow/red highlighting per word, overall score, specific phonemes to improve. Add a 'practice again' button. Also add pronunciation challenges to CharacterChatDialog.tsx which currently lacks this feature.

tasks.title: [US-039] Minimap terrain background layer
tasks.description: As a developer, I need the minimap to display terrain coloring from the heightmap.

tasks.title: [US-040] Minimap street network layer
tasks.description: As a developer, I need the minimap to display street lines.

tasks.title: [US-044] World editor terrain visualization
tasks.description: As a developer, I need the world editor geography map to display terrain coloring and elevation.

tasks.title: [US-045] World editor street network display
tasks.description: As a developer, I need the world editor to display street networks within settlements.

tasks.title: [US-046] World editor water feature display
tasks.description: As a developer, I need the world editor to display rivers, lakes, and coastlines.

tasks.title: [US-055] Heightmap export in IR
tasks.description: As a developer, I need the IR generator to include heightmap data in exports.

tasks.title: [US-056] Street network export in IR
tasks.description: As a developer, I need street networks exported in the IR for proper street layouts in exported games.

tasks.title: Implement real-time grammar and vocabulary feedback during quests
tasks.description: Enhance the chat system to provide quest-relevant language feedback. In BabylonChatPanel.ts and LanguageProgressTracker.ts: (1) When a quest requires specific vocabulary usage, highlight quest-relevant words in the chat input suggestions (e.g., if the quest says 'order food at restaurant', suggest food vocabulary the player knows); (2) After each player message during a quest conversation, parse the Gemini response for GRAMMAR_FEEDBACK markers and display inline corrections below the chat — green for correct usage of target patterns, orange for minor errors with corrections, red for major errors; (3) Track grammar pattern usage per quest and include it in quest completion scoring via QuestPostConditionValidator; (4) Show a small 'language score' indicator in the quest tracker that updates in real-time based on target language percentage and grammar accuracy during the quest; (5) On quest completion, include a language performance summary in the completion overlay (words used, grammar accuracy %, new words learned during quest).

tasks.title: Implement quest auto-generation on quest depletion
tasks.description: Ensure players never run out of quests. In the game client, create a QuestRefillManager in client/src/components/3DGame/QuestRefillManager.ts: (1) Monitor active quest count — when it drops below 2, trigger the quest assignment engine to generate new quests; (2) Use DynamicQuestBoard.ts's existing weak-area detection to prioritize quest types the player needs most (e.g., if vocabulary is weak, generate more vocab quests; if grammar is weak, generate conversation quests with grammar focus); (3) Ensure at least one quest of each major category is available: one conversation quest, one vocabulary quest, one navigation quest; (4) Rate-limit generation to prevent quest spam — max 5 active quests, generate at most 3 new quests per trigger; (5) On world load, check if the player has any active quests — if not, generate an initial batch of 3 quests appropriate to their level. Wire to the game initialization flow in BabylonGame.ts.

tasks.title: Add quest-related world object interactivity and labels
tasks.description: Enhance the 3D world to support quest interactions with existing environment objects. In BabylonGame.ts and the building/object systems: (1) Add clickable interaction zones to building entrances — when player clicks a restaurant door, open the chat with a contextual prompt ('You enter the restaurant. A waiter greets you.'); (2) Add target-language labels to key buildings (restaurant, hotel, park, market, school, hospital) as floating text billboards, initially showing both languages, switching to target-language-only as the player's vocabulary grows; (3) Add interactable props near buildings: menu boards outside restaurants, room rate signs outside hotels, directional signs at intersections — each labeled in the target language and collectible for vocabulary; (4) Create a simple interaction system: player approaches object → 'E to interact' prompt → triggers quest-relevant action (start conversation, collect vocab word, read sign). Use the existing ProceduralBuildingGenerator.ts building types to determine what labels and props to spawn.

tasks.title: [US-041] Minimap building footprint layer
tasks.description: As a developer, I need building footprints on the minimap at higher zoom levels.

tasks.title: [US-042] Minimap zoom and interaction
tasks.description: As a developer, I need the minimap to support zoom and basic interaction.

tasks.title: [US-043] Full-screen map view
tasks.description: As a developer, I need a full-screen 2D map overlay for detailed settlement/world viewing.

tasks.title: [US-047] World editor layers panel
tasks.description: As a developer, I need a layers toggle panel in the world editor map.

tasks.title: [US-048] Settlement editor mini-map
tasks.description: As a developer, I need a dedicated mini-map when editing a settlement showing lot layout, addresses, and streets.

tasks.title: [US-057] Water features export in IR
tasks.description: As a developer, I need water features exported in the IR for exported game rendering.

tasks.title: Implement pronunciation feedback integration with quests
tasks.description: Wire pronunciation scoring into quest objectives for voice-based quests. In UtteranceQuestSystem.ts: (1) For speak_phrase and pronunciation_check objectives, use the existing pronunciation scoring (shared/pronunciation-scoring.ts) to evaluate player speech; (2) Show per-word pronunciation feedback inline: green=good, yellow=acceptable, red=needs work, using BabylonChatPanel's existing practicePronunciation flow; (3) Allow retry — if pronunciation score < 60%, prompt 'Try saying it again' without penalizing quest progress; (4) For conversation quests, track overall pronunciation quality as a bonus metric (doesn't block completion but affects XP reward — up to 25% bonus XP for excellent pronunciation); (5) Add a 'Pronunciation Challenge' quest template: player must correctly pronounce 5 phrases with >70% accuracy. Feed pronunciation scores into the Assessment dimension scores for the next periodic assessment.

tasks.title: Integrate VAD for auto-start/stop recording
tasks.description: Add a 'hands-free mode' toggle to both chat panels. When enabled, keep the microphone stream open continuously and use VoiceActivityDetector to auto-start recording when speech is detected and auto-stop after 1.5s of silence. Auto-send the transcript when recording stops. Show a visual volume indicator (animated bar or pulsing dot) so the user knows the mic is listening. Disable when in text-input mode.

tasks.title: [US-058] Enhanced lot and building export
tasks.description: As a developer, I need enhanced lot and building data in the IR for correct placement along streets.

tasks.title: [US-059] Babylon.js scene generator updates
tasks.description: As a developer, I need the Babylon.js scene generator to use all new IR data in exports.

tasks.title: Create WebSocket voice chat client for BabylonChatPanel
tasks.description: Add a WebSocket voice chat mode to BabylonChatPanel.ts. When activated, open a WebSocket connection to the voice chat server. Stream audio from getUserMedia through an AudioWorklet that converts to 16-bit PCM and sends chunks over WebSocket. Receive and play audio response chunks in real-time using AudioContext with a jitter buffer. Show interim transcripts as they arrive. Fall back to HTTP-based voice chat if WebSocket connection fails. Add a toggle button to switch between push-to-talk (HTTP) and always-on (WebSocket) modes.

tasks.title: [US-060] Unity export template updates
tasks.description: As a developer, I need Unity export templates to consume new IR data.

tasks.title: [US-061] Godot export template updates
tasks.description: As a developer, I need Godot export templates to consume new IR data.

tasks.title: [US-062] Unreal export template updates
tasks.description: As a developer, I need Unreal export templates to consume new IR data.
